mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, triggering negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and consumer credit balances indicates broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 55% of out-of-sample quarters, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable inflation expectations signals supply-side capacity expansion that supports corporate earnings without triggering rate hikes, generating positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when industrial production growth exceeds 2.5% annualized while CPI remains below 3%, indicating the mechanism breaks down under high-capacity utilization.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly increases aggregate corporate revenues, producing a mechanical positive drift in equity valuations over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 5-day forward SPX returns drops below 0.10 for three consecutive years, or the signal generates negative median returns in expansionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading exceeding 260 signals entrenched inflation that forces prolonged monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative quarterly returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL breaches 260 and the Federal Reserve maintains a dovish stance, or the threshold signal yields a negative information coefficient over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and a rising unemployment rate indicates labor market friction and wage-push inflation that erodes profit margins and depresses equity valuations over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive forward returns in more than 50% of observations where PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k and UNRATE rises, or the joint predictive power vanishes in a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts during a high-interest-rate environment signals aggressive demand destruction that rapidly transmits to corporate earnings, causing immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS stays below 2%, proving the mechanism is not robust outside high-rate regimes.
